content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify real defects in the planned-elsewhere feature. Amendment fr-10-91f7448a correctly identifies that reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings scans ALL project-level final-review aggregates without scoping to the current run, risking stale mapping resurrection. Amendment fr-10-872f23a9 correctly identifies that the reviewer template never mentions planned-elsewhere classification or mapped_to_bead_id, so reviewers have no guidance to produce the field — only the JSON schema hint exists.

## Votes

- `fr-10-91f7448a`: Accept
  The defect is real and consequential. `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` calls `FsArtifactStore.list_payloads(base_dir, &pid)` which returns ALL payloads for the entire project across all runs and all completion rounds. The function then iterates every `FinalReview` / `StageAggregate` record without filtering by run ID or completion round. In a project with multiple runs (e.g., after restarts or re-runs), stale aggregates from superseded runs contain outdated planned-elsewhere decisions that may reference beads that have since been closed, reprioritized, or had their scope change. Reconstructing these stale mappings and persisting them to the journal (line 941) and then acting on them during reconciliation is a data integrity bug. The payload_id already encodes the run identifier (e.g., `run-20260409075617-...`), so scoping the reconstruction to the current run is straightforward. The dedup by identity key (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) is insufficient protection because the same finding text could legitimately map to different beads across runs, or a mapping that was correct in an older run may be incorrect now.
- `fr-10-872f23a9`: Accept
  The defect is real. The `FINAL_REVIEW_REVIEWER_DEFAULT` template (template_catalog.rs:220) contains zero guidance about planned-elsewhere classification or the `mapped_to_bead_id` field. Reviewers see the field in the JSON schema (via `FinalReviewProposal`'s `JsonSchema` derive) but have no instructions on when to use it, what bead ID format to use, or how to determine which bead owns a concern. The planned-elsewhere prompt text was only added to the voter (line 1880) and arbiter (line 1937) prompts, which render it as informational context on amendments that already have `mapped_to_bead_id` set — but by then it's too late, the field needed to be produced upstream by the reviewer. The merge logic in `merge_final_review_amendments` (line 167) handles the field conservatively (clearing on disagreement), which is correct behavior, but it can only work if reviewers actually produce the field. Without reviewer instructions, `mapped_to_bead_id` will almost always be `None`, making the entire planned-elsewhere feature effectively dead at the reviewer stage. The reviewer prompt needs explicit instructions: what planned-elsewhere means, how to identify it, what bead ID to reference, and when to set vs. omit the field.
